label: AWS RDS MySQL
description: Connect to an AWS-managed MySQL database instance via its RDS endpoint.

form.title: Connect AWS RDS MySQL
form.subtitle: Enter your RDS endpoint and credentials to connect Anton to your MySQL instance.

methods.label: Direct Connection
methods.description: Paste your RDS endpoint, port, database name, and MySQL credentials. The simplest path — no extra tooling required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via RDS Endpoint Credentials

Find your RDS endpoint and connect with your MySQL username and password.

### Steps

1. Open the [AWS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/) and navigate to **RDS → Databases**.
2. Click the DB instance you want to connect to.
3. Under the **Connectivity & security** tab, locate the **Endpoint** (e.g. `mydb.abc123xyz.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com`) and **Port** (default: `3306`).
4. Copy the endpoint and paste it into the **Host** field below.
5. Enter the **Port** (leave as `3306` unless you changed it).
6. Enter the **Database** name you want Anton to connect to.
7. Enter the **Username** and **Password** for a MySQL user that has the required privileges.
8. Toggle **Use SSL** on (recommended) — AWS RDS enforces SSL by default on most instance types.

### Notes

- Make sure your RDS security group allows inbound traffic on port 3306 from the machine running Anton. Go to **RDS → Databases → your instance → Connectivity & security → VPC security groups** and add an inbound rule if needed.
- If your instance is in a private VPC, you may need a bastion host or VPN to reach it from your local machine.
- The MySQL user should have at minimum `SELECT` privileges on the target database, or broader privileges if Anton needs to write data.
- SSL certificates for RDS are available at the [AWS SSL/TLS documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/UsingWithRDS.SSL.html).

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host (RDS Endpoint)
fields.description: Found in AWS Console → RDS → Databases → your instance → Connectivity & security tab.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: MySQL default is 3306. Change only if your instance uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the specific MySQL database (schema) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: MySQL user with access to the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the MySQL user above. Stored encrypted locally.

fields.name: use_ssl
fields.label: Use SSL
fields.description: Encrypt the connection with SSL/TLS. Strongly recommended — AWS RDS enforces SSL by default on most instance types.

methods.label: IAM Database Authentication
methods.description: Use AWS IAM credentials to generate short-lived authentication tokens instead of a static password. Requires IAM DB auth enabled on the RDS instance.
methods.how_to:
## IAM Database Authentication

AWS RDS supports passwordless login using short-lived IAM tokens. This avoids storing a static MySQL password.

### Prerequisites

- IAM DB authentication must be enabled on your RDS instance.
- Your IAM user or role must have the `rds-db:connect` permission.

### Steps

1. Open **AWS Console → RDS → Databases** and click your instance.
2. Under **Configuration**, confirm **IAM database authentication** is set to **Enabled**. If not, click **Modify** to enable it.
3. In **IAM → Policies**, create or attach a policy granting `rds-db:connect` on the target DB resource ARN (e.g. `arn:aws:rds-db:us-east-1:123456789012:dbuser:db-ABCDEFG/myuser`).
4. In **IAM → Users (or Roles) → Security credentials**, generate an **Access key ID** and **Secret access key** for the IAM identity Anton will use.
5. In your RDS MySQL instance, create a database user with the `AWSAuthenticationPlugin`:
   - `CREATE USER 'myuser'@'%' IDENTIFIED WITH AWSAuthenticationPlugin AS 'RDS';`
   - Grant appropriate privileges to this user.
6. Paste the IAM **Access Key ID**, **Secret Access Key**, **AWS Region**, and the remaining connection details below.

### Notes

- IAM tokens expire after 15 minutes; Anton will automatically generate a new token for each connection attempt.
- SSL is required for IAM auth — the toggle is forced on.
- The IAM user needs network-level access to the RDS endpoint just like standard auth.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host (RDS Endpoint)
fields.description: Found in AWS Console → RDS → Databases → your instance → Connectivity & security tab.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the specific MySQL database (schema) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: db_username
fields.label: Database Username
fields.description: The MySQL user created with AWSAuthenticationPlugin. Must match the IAM policy resource ARN.

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: AWS Access Key ID
fields.description: IAM user access key ID with rds-db:connect permission on this instance.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: AWS Secret Access Key
fields.description: Secret access key paired with the Access Key ID above.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region your RDS instance is deployed in (e.g. us-east-1, eu-west-2).
